description: Every =-link in each chain holds: the limit equals 2x, the integral chain equals 2/3, and the antiderivative of 1/(x+1) is ln(x+1) + C
\frac instead of /, braces around multi-character exponents like x^{3/2}, \left[ \right] for the evaluation bracket, \, before dx
Chains show the derivation (integral = bracket = answer), not just a bare final result
Text and math read well together; exercise markers kept; no leftover placeholder comments
